brief_document.title: Fix server config file and all README startup instructions
brief_document.purpose: The entire getting-started path is broken. dev-config.json is JSON but the server only parses TOML (changed by AO-003). The file is passed as a positional arg but the CLI requires --config. The field names don't match ServerConfig. All three example READMEs reference this broken file. No developer can get past 'start the server' without reading source code.
brief_document.task: Replace dev-config.json with a valid dev-config.toml that matches the ServerConfig struct. Update every README and GETTING-STARTED.md to use the correct --config flag and the new file. Document the aion.toml auto-discovery feature. Ensure a developer can copy-paste the startup commands and have the server running.

requirements.title: Replace dev-config.json with valid TOML config
requirements.spec: THE SYSTEM SHALL provide a dev-config.toml at the repo root whose fields match the ServerConfig struct exactly. The file SHALL use memory store backend, default namespace, and listen on 127.0.0.1:50051 (gRPC) and 127.0.0.1:8080 (HTTP). THE SYSTEM SHALL delete the broken dev-config.json.
acceptance: dev-config.toml exists at repo root and is valid TOML.
cargo run -p aion-server -- --config dev-config.toml starts the server without error.
The config includes workflow_packages pointing to examples/hello-world/hello-world.aion if the file exists.

requirements.title: Fix all README startup commands
requirements.spec: WHEN a developer follows GETTING-STARTED.md or any example README, THE SYSTEM SHALL provide copy-pasteable commands that work without modification. All references to dev-config.json SHALL be replaced with dev-config.toml and the --config flag.
acceptance: GETTING-STARTED.md server start command works when copy-pasted.
examples/hello-world/README.md server start command works when copy-pasted.
examples/order-saga/README.md references the correct config file.
examples/agent-orchestration/README.md references the correct config file.
No file in the repo references dev-config.json.

requirements.title: Document aion.toml auto-discovery
requirements.spec: GETTING-STARTED.md SHALL mention that the server auto-loads aion.toml from the working directory when --config is not provided.
acceptance: A developer placing aion.toml in the working directory can start the server with no flags.

boundaries: SHALL NOT change the server CLI parser or config parsing logic — only fix the config file and docs.
SHALL NOT add new config features — only align existing docs with existing code.
verification: cargo run -p aion-server -- --config dev-config.toml starts without error.
All README startup commands work when copy-pasted.
